meta.revision_note: Part 1: na.sa (H5375O) and da.var (H1697H) have no verse records by researcher decision. Phase 2 note recorded in data_quality_flags for both terms.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) pleasure, delight, favour, goodwill, acceptance, will

1b) acceptance

desire, delight, beauty, desirable, pleasant
desiring, delighting in, having pleasure in

desirableness, preciousness

desire, thing desired

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:

1b) (Pual) fillet
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: CONSOLIDATION_CANDIDATE: che.sheq (H2837), cha.shaq H2836A, and cha.shaq H2836B are three STEP entries sharing the same root. Verses for H2836B are the tabernacle fillet passages (Exo 27:17, 38:17, 38:28) — architectural/literal sense only.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: (Qal) to love, be attached to, long for
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: Shares CHASHAQ root with H2837 and H2836B. Inner-being sense (love, attachment, longing) is primary here. god_as_subject=true: Deu 7:7, 10:15 — God's love/attachment to Israel.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: desire

1) desire
1a) desire, wish, longings of one's heart
1a1) lust, appetite, covetousness (bad sense)
1b) thing desired, object of desire

1) desire, longing, craving
1a) of man for woman
1b) of woman for man
1c) of beast to devour
(sensuous) love
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional H5689 corrected to H5690.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: entreat, longing, desire (particle interjection)

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: possession

1) to ask, seek, request, desire, pray, make petition
1a) (P'al)
1a1) to ask, request

term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: Aramaic term (Daniel, Ezra). Desire/petition sense.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to ask, enquire, borrow, beg
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to ask, ask for
1a2) to ask (as a favour), borrow
1a3) to enquire, enquire of
1a4) to enquire of, consult (of deity, oracle)
1a5) to seek
1b) (Niphal) to ask for oneself, ask leave of absence
1c) (Piel)
1c1) to enquire, enquire carefully
1c2) to beg, practise beggary
1d) (Hiphil)
1d1) to be given on request
1d2) to grant, make over to, let (one) ask (successfully) or give or lend on request
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 179 occurrences; verses selected by researcher covering representative range.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to choose, elect, decide for
1a) (Qal) to choose
1b) (Niphal) to be chosen
1c) (Pual) to be chosen, selected
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 171 occurrences; selected verses cover the key 'God chooses' and 'human chooses' senses.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) desire, incline, covet, wait longingly, wish, sigh, want, be greedy, prefer
1a) (Piel) to desire, crave (food and drink)
1b) (Hithpael) to desire, long for, lust after (of bodily appetites)

1) to desire, covet, take pleasure in, delight in
1a) (Qal) to desire
1b) (Niphal) to be desirable
1c) (Piel) to delight greatly, desire greatly
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional H2530 corrected to H2530A.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
: trust/desire/lift(soul/heart)
1) to lift, bear up, carry, take
1a) (Qal): to lift up; to bear, carry, support, sustain, endure; to take, take away, carry off, forgive
1b) (Niphal): to be lifted up, be exalted; to lift oneself up, rise up; to be borne, carried; to be taken away
1c) (Piel): to lift up, exalt, support, aid, assist; to desire, long (fig.); to carry, bear continuously; to take away
1d) (Hithpael) to lift oneself up, exalt oneself
1e) (Hiphil): to cause one to bear (iniquity); to cause to bring, have brought
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: NO_VERSES: Researcher deliberately omitted verses (659 occurrences across 20+ sub-glosses; only the trust/desire sub-gloss H5375O is relevant here). PHASE 2 NOTE: If Session B analysis of desire produces weak conclusions or unanswered questions regarding this term, the researcher must conduct additional research on na.sa independently before drawing synthesis-level conclusions.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to long for, yearn for, long after
1a) (Qal) to long for
1b) (Niphal): to long for (deeply); being longed for (participle)

1) to gasp, pant, pant after, long for, breathe heavily
1a) (Qal): to gasp, pant after, snuff up; to gasp or pant (with desire), pant after, be eager for; to thirst for one's blood (from actions of animals) (fig)
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: somatic_link=true: panting/gasping as physical expression of desire.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
: lover
1) to love
1a) (Qal): human love for another, includes family, and sexual; human appetite for objects such as food, drink, sleep, wisdom; human love for or to God; act of being a friend; lover (participle); friend (participle); God's love toward man
1b) (Niphal): lovely (participle); loveable (participle)
1c) (Piel): friends; lovers (fig. of adulterers)
2) to like
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: source confirms H0157G (lover sub-gloss). SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 211 occurrences; representative verses selected.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to seek, require, desire, exact, request
1a) (Piel): to seek to find; to seek to secure; to seek the face; to desire, demand; to require, exact; to ask, request
1b) (Pual) to be sought
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 225 occurrences; selected verses cover key desire/seeking senses.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: (Qal) to spare, pity, have compassion on

1) to desire, be inclined, be willing, be pleased
1a) (P'al): to desire; to be pleased; to will (without hindrance) (of God)
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional H6633 corrected to H6634. Aramaic term (Daniel). god_as_subject=true: Dan 4:17, 4:25, 4:32, 4:35 — God gives kingdoms to whom he wills.

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
: speaking
1) speech, word, speaking, thing
1a) speech
1b) saying, utterance
1c) word, words
1d) business, occupation, acts, matter, case, something, manner (by extension)
term_inventory_hebrew.status_note: NO_VERSES: 1381 occurrences across multiple sub-glosses; researcher deliberately omitted verses due to volume and peripheral semantic fit to desire. Only the 'speaking' sub-gloss H1697H is included here. PHASE 2 NOTE: If Session B analysis of desire produces weak conclusions or unanswered questions regarding this term, the researcher must conduct additional research on da.var independently before drawing synthesis-level conclusions.

verse_records.verse_text: For in their anger they killed men, and in their willfulness they hamstrung oxen.

It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear any guilt from the holy things that the people of Israel consecrate as their holy gifts. It shall regularly be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd, he shall offer a male without blemish. He shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, that he may be accepted before the LORD.
When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD, you shall offer it so that you may be accepted.
if it is to be accepted for you it shall be a male without blemish, of the bulls or the sheep or the goats.
You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable for you.

And when anyone offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering from the herd or from the flock, to be accepted it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.
and he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD, so that you may be accepted. On the day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.
with the best gifts of the earth and its fullness and the favor of him who dwells in the bush. May these rest on the head of Joseph, on the pate of him who is prince among his brothers.
O Naphtali, sated with favor, and full of the blessing of the LORD, possess the lake and the south.
And all Judah rejoiced over the oath, for they had sworn with all their heart and had sought him with their whole desire, and he was found by them, and the LORD gave them rest all around.
Now then make confession to the LORD, the God of your fathers and do his will. Separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign wives.
And its rich yield goes to the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins. They rule over our bodies and over our livestock as they please, and we are in great distress.
And drinking was according to this edict: 'There is no compulsion.' For the king had given orders to all the staff of his palace to do as each man desired.
The Jews struck all their enemies with the sword, killing and destroying them, and did as they pleased to those who hated them.
For you bless the righteous, O LORD; you cover him with favor as with a shield.
Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.
For his anger is but for a moment, and his favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning.
I delight to do your will, O my God; your law is within my heart.
Do good to Zion in your good pleasure; build up the walls of Jerusalem;
But as for me, my prayer is to you, O LORD. At an acceptable time, O God, in the abundance of your steadfast love answer me in your saving faithfulness.
For you are the glory of their strength; by your favor our horn is exalted.
Bless the LORD, all his hosts, his ministers, who do his will!
Remember me, O LORD, when you show favor to your people; help me when you save them,
Teach me to do your will, for you are my God! Let your good Spirit lead me on level ground!
You open your hand; you satisfy the desire of every living thing.
He fulfills the desire of those who fear him; he also hears their cry and saves them.
For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the LORD,
A false balance is an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is his delight.
Those of crooked heart are an abomination to the LORD, but those of blameless ways are his delight.
Fools mock at the guilt offering, but the upright enjoy acceptance.
A servant who deals wisely has the king's favor, but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully.
Righteous lips are the delight of a king, and he loves him who speaks what is right.
He who finds a wife finds a good thing and obtains favor from the LORD.
Thus says the LORD: In a time of favor I have answered you; in a day of salvation I have helped you.
I saw the ram charging westward and northward and southward. No beast could stand before him, and there was no one who could rescue from his power. He did as he pleased and became great.
And the king shall do as he wills. He shall exalt himself and magnify himself above every god, and shall speak astonishing things against the God of gods.
And this second thing you do. You cover the LORD'S altar with tears, with weeping and groaning because he no longer regards the offering or accepts it with favor from your hand.
In that day, 'A pleasant vineyard, sing of it!'
Beat your breasts for the pleasant fields, for the fruitful vine,
clothed in purple, governors and commanders, all of them desirable young men, horsemen riding on horses.
She lusted after the Assyrians, governors and commanders, warriors clothed in full armor, horsemen riding on horses, all of them desirable young men.

the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans, Pekod and Shoa and Koa, and all the Assyrians with them, desirable young men, governors and commanders all of them, officers and men of renown, all of them riding on horses.

Therefore because you trample on the poor and you exact taxes of grain from him, you have built houses of hewn stone, but you shall not dwell in them; you have planted pleasant vineyards, but you shall not drink their wine.
After this thing Jeroboam did not turn from his evil way, but made priests for the high places again from among all the people. Any who would, he ordained to be priests of the high places.

And he said to her, 'Because I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite and said to him, Give me your vineyard for money, or else, if it please you, I will give you another vineyard for it. And he answered, I will not give you my vineyard.'
And you, Solomon my son, know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and with a willing mind, for the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought.
O Lord, let your ear be attentive to the prayer of your servant, and to the prayer of your servants who delight to fear your name.
For you are not a God who delights in wickedness; evil may not dwell with you.
What man is there who desires life and loves many days, that he may see good?
Let those who delight in my righteousness shout for joy and be glad and say evermore, 'Great is the Lord, who delights in the welfare of his servant!'
Let those be put to shame and disappointed altogether who seek to snatch away my life.
Behold, you delight in truth in the inward being, and you teach me wisdom in the secret heart.
For you will not delight in sacrifice, or I would give it; you will not be pleased with a burnt offering.
then will you delight in right sacrifices, in burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings; then bulls will be offered on your altar.
Blessed is the man who fears the LORD, who greatly delights in his commandments!
Our God is in the heavens; he does all that he pleases.
Lead me in the path of your commandments, for I delight in it.
Whatever the LORD pleases, he does, in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all deeps.
His delight is not in the strength of the horse, nor his pleasure in the legs of a man,
And the young man did not delay to do the thing, because he delighted in Jacob's daughter.
If the LORD delights in us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
But if you no longer delight in her, you shall let her go where she wants.

If someone sins against a man, God will mediate for him, but if someone sins against the LORD, who can intercede for him? But they would not listen to the voice of their father, for it was the will of the LORD to put them to death.
And Saul commanded his servants, 'Speak to David in private and say, Behold, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now then become the king's son-in-law.'
And Saul spoke to Jonathan his son and to all his servants, that they should kill David. But Jonathan, Saul's son, delighted much in David.
But if he says, 'I have no pleasure in you,' behold, here I am, let him do to me what seems good to him.
He brought me out into a broad place; he rescued me, because he delighted in me.
He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him; let him rescue him, for he delights in him!
The steps of a man are established by the LORD, when he delights in his way;
In sacrifice and offering you have not delighted, but you have given me an open ear.
By this I know that you delight in me: my enemy will not shout in triumph over me.
Rebuke the beasts that dwell among the reeds, the herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples. Trample underfoot those who lust after tribute; scatter the peoples who delight in war.
Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you.
He loved to curse; let curses come upon him! He did not delight in blessing; may it be far from him!
If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand times.
But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
They say to God, 'Depart from us! We do not desire the knowledge of your ways.'

What to me is the multitude of your sacrifices? says the LORD; I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of well-fed beasts; I do not delight in the blood of bulls, or of lambs, or of goats.
The LORD was pleased, for his righteousness' sake, to magnify his law and make it glorious.
Yet it was the will of the LORD to crush him; he has put him to grief.
Yet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways, as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their God.
You shall no more be termed Forsaken, and your land shall no more be termed Desolate, but you shall be called My Delight Is in Her, and your land Married; for the LORD delights in you.
He who slaughters an ox is like one who kills a man; he who sacrifices a lamb, like one who breaks a dog's neck. These have chosen their own ways, and their soul delights in their abominations.

but let him who boasts boast in this, that he understands and knows me, that I am the LORD who practices steadfast love, justice, and righteousness in the earth. For in these things I delight, declares the LORD.
Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked, declares the Lord GOD, and not rather that he should turn from his way and live?
For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone, declares the Lord GOD; so turn, and live.
Say to them, As I live, declares the Lord GOD, I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but that the wicked turn from his way and live.
For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings.
Who is a God like you, pardoning iniquity and passing over transgression for the remnant of his inheritance? He does not retain his anger forever, because he delights in steadfast love.

Behold, I send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me. And the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple; and the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight, behold, he is coming, says the LORD of hosts.

Jerusalem remembers in the days of her affliction and wandering all the precious things that were hers from days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the foe, and there was none to help her, her foes gloated over her; they mocked at her downfall.
Nevertheless I will send my servants to you tomorrow about this time, and they shall search your house and the houses of your servants and lay hands on whatever pleases you and take it away.
And they burned the house of God and broke down the wall of Jerusalem and burned all its palaces with fire and destroyed all its precious vessels.
His mouth is most sweet, and he is altogether desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
Our holy and beautiful house, where our fathers praised you, has been burned by fire, and all our pleasant places have become ruins.
The enemy has stretched out his hands over all her precious things.
All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength.
He has bent his bow like an enemy, with his right hand set like a foe; and he has killed all who were delightful in our eyes.
Son of man, behold, I am about to take the delight of your eyes away from you at a stroke; yet you shall not mourn or weep, nor shall your tears run down.
Say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord GOD: Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the delight of your eyes, and the yearning of your soul.
As for you, son of man, surely on the day when I take from them their stronghold, their joy and glory, the delight of their eyes and their soul's desire.
Nettles shall possess their precious things of silver; thorns shall be in their tents.
Even though they give birth, I will put their beloved children to death.
For you have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried my rich treasures into your temples.
Then Rebekah took the best garments of Esau her older son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son.
At the beginning of your pleas for mercy a word went out, and I have come to tell it to you, for you are greatly loved.
I ate no delicacies, no meat or wine entered my mouth, nor did I anoint myself at all, for the full three weeks.

And he said to me, 'O Daniel, man greatly loved, understand the words that I speak to you, and stand upright, for now I have been sent to you.' And when he had spoken this word to me, I stood up trembling.
And he said, 'O man greatly loved, fear not, peace be with you; be strong and of good courage.' And as he spoke to me, I was strengthened and said, 'Let my lord speak, for you have strengthened me.'

As for your donkeys that were lost three days ago, do not set your mind on them, for they have been found. And for whom is all that is desirable in Israel? Is it not for you and for all your father's house?
He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem. And he departed with no one's regret.
And Hezekiah had very great riches and honor, and he made for himself treasuries for silver, for gold, for precious stones, for spices, for shields, and for all kinds of costly vessels;
In the spring of the year King Nebuchadnezzar sent and brought him to Babylon, with the precious vessels of the house of the Lord, and made his brother Zedekiah king over Judah and Jerusalem.
Then they despised the pleasant land, having no faith in his promise.
against all the ships of Tarshish, and against all the beautiful craft.
I said, How I would set you among my sons, and give you a pleasant land, a heritage most beautiful of all nations.
Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard; they have trampled down my portion; they have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness.
Wail, you shepherds, and cry out, and roll in ashes, you lords of the flock, for the days of your slaughter and dispersion have come, and you shall fall like a choice vessel.
They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise. They will break down your walls and destroy your pleasant houses.
He shall also carry off to Egypt their gods with their metal images and their precious vessels of silver and gold.
He shall pay no attention to the gods of his fathers, or to the one beloved by women.

Though he may flourish among his brothers, the east wind, the wind of the Lord, shall come, rising from the wilderness, and his fountain shall dry up; his spring shall be parched; it shall strip his treasury of every precious thing.
Plunder the silver, plunder the gold! There is no end of the treasure or of the wealth of all precious things.
And I will shake all nations, so that the treasures of all nations shall come in, and I will fill this house with glory, says the Lord of hosts.
and I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations that they had not known. Thus the land they left was desolate, so that no one went to and fro, and the pleasant land was made desolate.
However, you may slaughter and eat meat within any of your towns, as much as you desire, according to the blessing of the Lord your God that he has given you.
When the Lord your God enlarges your territory, as he has promised you, and you say, 'I will eat meat,' because you crave meat, you may eat meat whenever you desire.

If the place that the Lord your God will choose to put his name there is too far from you, then you may kill any of your herd or your flock, which the Lord has given you, as I have commanded you, and you may eat within your towns whenever you desire.
And if a Levite comes from any of your towns out of all Israel, where he lives—and he may come when he desires—to the place that the Lord will choose,
Now come down, O king, according to all your heart's desire to come down, and our part shall be to surrender him into the king's hand.
a wild donkey used to the wilderness, in her heat sniffing the wind! Who can restrain her lust? None who seek her need weary themselves; in her month they will find her.
When I please, I will discipline them, and nations shall be gathered against them when they are bound up for their double iniquity.
Oh that my vexation were weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!
Is there any injustice on my tongue? Cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity?
They break up my path; they promote my calamity; they need no one to help them.
For there is no truth in their mouth; their inmost self is destruction; their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
Those who seek my life lay their snares; those who seek my hurt speak of ruin and meditate treachery all day long.
Your tongue plots destruction, like a sharp razor, you worker of deceit.
See the man who would not make God his refuge, but trusted in the abundance of his riches and sought refuge in his own destruction!
ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me, for in you my soul takes refuge; in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge, till the storms of destruction pass by.
For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler and from the deadly pestilence.
The LORD does not let the righteous go hungry, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their lust.
Their hands are on what is evil, to do it well; the prince and the judge ask for a bribe, and the great man utters the evil desire of his soul; thus they weave it together.
As soon as Solomon had finished building the house of the Lord and the king's house and all that Solomon desired to build,

and all the store cities that Solomon had, and the cities for his chariots, and the cities for his horsemen, and whatever Solomon desired to build in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.

and Baalath, and all the store cities that Solomon had and all the cities for his chariots and the cities for his horsemen, and whatever Solomon desired to build in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.
My heart staggers; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
All the pillars around the court shall be filleted with silver. Their hooks shall be of silver, and their bases of bronze.
And the bases for the pillars were of bronze, but the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
And of the 1,775 shekels he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their capitals and made fillets for them.
But Hamor spoke with them, saying, 'The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him to be his wife.'
It was not because you were more in number than any other people that the LORD set his love on you and chose you, for you were the fewest of all peoples,
Yet the LORD set his heart in love on your fathers and chose their offspring after them, you above all peoples, as you are this day.
and you see among the captives a beautiful woman, and you desire to take her to be your wife,

and all the store cities that Solomon had, and the cities for his chariots, and the cities for his horsemen, and whatever Solomon desired to build in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.

and Baalath, and all the store cities that Solomon had and all the cities for his chariots and the cities for his horsemen, and whatever Solomon desired to build in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.
Because he holds fast to me in love, I will deliver him; I will protect him, because he knows my name.
Behold, it was for my welfare that I had great bitterness; but in love you have delivered my life from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
Grant not, O Lord, the desires of the wicked; do not further their evil plot, or they will be exalted! Selah

So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate.
Now the rabble that was among them had a strong craving. And the people of Israel also wept again and said, 'Oh that we had meat to eat!'
so that his life loathes bread, and his appetite the choicest food.
For the wicked boasts of the desires of his soul, and the one greedy for gain curses and renounces the LORD.
O LORD, you hear the desire of the afflicted; you will strengthen their heart; you will incline your ear

cross_registry_links.note: a.viy.yo.nah (H0060) is owned by sorrow (154). Listed as XREF in Part 1.
Comfort/consolation sense of na.cham (registry 135 repentance) and parakaleō (G3870) overlap with grief (071) in the comfort-response context.
Desire and will vocabulary (epithumeō, thelō, ba.char) overlaps with repentance register in turning/choosing contexts.
cha.mad (H2530A) appears in the 10th commandment (Exo 20:17; Deu 5:21) — core guilt/coveting intersection.
eudokia (goodwill/pleasure) and ra.tson (favor/acceptance/will) both appear in peace contexts (Luk 2:14; Psa 30:5).
prosdokia (G4329, owned by anxiety 007) overlaps with fear registry in apprehension/expectation sense.
cha.phets (delighting in, H2654A/H2655) and eudokia co-occur with joy vocabulary across Psalms and NT.
a.hev (H0157G) is the primary Hebrew love term and sits within desire registry under the 'lover' sub-gloss. The desire-love boundary will be a significant Phase 2 and synthesis question.

ma.a.vay occurs about 1x. Single verse record (Psa 140:8). Assessments will be limited.

epizēteō occurrence count not stated in source. Selected verse coverage only.

ba.char occurs about 171x. 20 verses recorded (~12% coverage). Selected to cover human and divine choosing.
a.hev occurs about 211x. 24 verses recorded (~11% coverage). Selected to cover human love, divine love, and lover/friend senses.
ba.qash occurs about 225x. 20 verses recorded (~9% coverage). Selected to cover seeking God, seeking life, and the demand sense.
cha.mal occurs about 41x. 12 verses recorded (~29% — above 20% threshold). Flagged for awareness.

che.sheq (H2837), cha.shaq to desire (H2836A), and cha.shaq to connect/fillet (H2836B) share the same root. H2836B is architectural (fillet sense, Exo tabernacle passages only) — likely excludable from inner-being analysis at Phase 2. Both H2836A and H2836B retained as separate entries pending Phase 2 review.

a.hev (to love: lover, H0157G) is a boundary term between desire and love registries. With 211 occurrences and sub-glosses for human love, divine love, and appetite/craving, it is under-extracted here. The love registry (TBD) will need to treat this as a primary owned term. Also_used_in for love registry should be added when that registry is processed.

cha.mal (to spare/pity/compassion) fits the desire word set peripherally — it is the opposite of desire in some contexts (not desiring to destroy). Its primary sense is pity/compassion rather than desire. Phase 2 should assess whether it contributes to the desire dataset.
